document_metadata.title: Lakeland cuts £40 off electric throw that shoppers say makes them 'snug as a bug'
document_metadata.first_words: Lakeland has launched its Black Friday event, featuring numerous reductions on winter-ready homeware, including electric blankets and throws. It's the ideal time to buy discounted heated throws, as temperatures are set to plummet this week. One of the biggest savings is on a StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw, which is reduced by £40 and is on sale for £49.99, from £89.99. content_metadata.document_title_txt: Lakeland cuts £40 off electric throw that shoppers say makes them 'snug as a bug'

chunks.content_original: Lakeland has launched its Black Friday event, featuring numerous reductions on winter-ready homeware, including electric blankets and throws. It's the ideal time to buy discounted heated throws, as temperatures are set to plummet this week. One of the biggest savings is on a StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw, which is reduced by £40 and is on sale for £49.99, from £89.99. The grey blanket has a soft fleece lining, nine heat settings and nine timer settings. Measuring 120cm x 160cm, it can be used on beds and sofas for cosy evenings. The soft flannel outer layer of the blanket has a velvety, silver sheen. The StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw features built-in overheating protection, allowing users to set the desired heat and time in advance. Similarly, Lakeland has also cut £10 off the StaySnug Velvety Taupe Heated Throw, now £29.99, was £39.99. Bedding brand Silentnight also has a variety of electric throws, including a grey Luxury Heated Throw now on sale for £36, down from £45 at Boots. Also measuring 120cm x 160cm, it has nine heat settings, including a pre-heat option. Meanwhile, this Cosi Home electric throw has more than 12,000 customer reviews on Amazon. On sale for £40.79, the reversible blanket features warm microfleece fabric and a Sherpa reverse. Lakeland's Grey Sherpa Electric Heated Throw has garnered glowing reviews from purchasers, many of whom have complimented its warmth and ease of use. One person said: “Snug as a bug. Lovely soft blanket that heats up well.“ Another shopper commented: “Excellent heat, great control. Lovely and cosy with fleece underside.“ However, not all consumers were happy. One purchaser said it heats up slowly and had expected 'more of an instant heat'. They continued: “The heat starts at the corners and then gradually works its way to the middle. The heat is slow but, over time, creates a subtle warmth. “The control handset is easy to navigate, although it would benefit from a longer lead. Overall, I would say that this heated throw is warmish but not hot. It is probably worth the purchase to offset against keeping the central heating thermostat down.“ An additional customer had a better experience, writing: “I can honestly say this is the best thing I’ve ever bought! My boiler had broken down, so I had no heating for a week. The heated throw arrived the next day and was a lifesaver! I have recommended it to everyone.“ A final buyer of the StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw concluded: “I bought this to save money on heating bills. In the evening, I can turn the heating off (or down if it's very cold) and use this to keep warm. It does the trick! It has 9 heat settings and, so far, I've only used the lowest setting. “It also has different options for automatic shut off. It's lovely and soft. I'm very pleased with it and with it being a Black Friday deal I got money off too. Highly recommended. There are different colours and styles available.“

raw_content:
frontpage - Devon Live Online https://www.devonlive.com/ RSS feed from Devon Live - frontpage Lakeland cuts £40 off electric throw that shoppers say makes them 'snug as a bug' https://www.devonlive.com/whats-on/shopping/lakeland-cuts-40-electric-throw-10649558 Tue, 18 Nov 2025 10:10:23 +0000 Lakeland's Black Friday sale is underway with significant deals on homeware Tue, 18 Nov 2025 10:10:23 GMT 10649558 Nisha Mal 2025-11-18T10:10:23Z Lakeland has launched its Black Friday event, featuring numerous reductions on winter-ready homeware, including electric blankets and throws. It's the ideal time to buy discounted heated throws, as temperatures are set to plummet this week.

One of the biggest savings is on a StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw, which is reduced by £40 and is on sale for £49.99, from £89.99. The grey blanket has a soft fleece lining, nine heat settings and nine timer settings.

Measuring 120cm x 160cm, it can be used on beds and sofas for cosy evenings. The soft flannel outer layer of the blanket has a velvety, silver sheen.

  • Shopping editor praises 'suitcase that never gets lost at airports' - now with 30% off
  • M&S 'go-to' cable knit jumper that's a 'perfect fit' is under £30 in sale

The StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw features built-in overheating protection, allowing users to set the desired heat and time in advance. Similarly, Lakeland has also cut £10 off the StaySnug Velvety Taupe Heated Throw, now £29.99, was £39.99.

Bedding brand Silentnight also has a variety of electric throws, including a grey Luxury Heated Throw now on sale for £36, down from £45 at Boots. Also measuring 120cm x 160cm, it has nine heat settings, including a pre-heat option.

Meanwhile, this Cosi Home electric throw has more than 12,000 customer reviews on Amazon. On sale for £40.79, the reversible blanket features warm microfleece fabric and a Sherpa reverse.

Lakeland's Grey Sherpa Electric Heated Throw has garnered glowing reviews from purchasers, many of whom have complimented its warmth and ease of use. One person said: "Snug as a bug. Lovely soft blanket that heats up well."

Another shopper commented: "Excellent heat, great control. Lovely and cosy with fleece underside."

However, not all consumers were happy. One purchaser said it heats up slowly and had expected 'more of an instant heat'. They continued: "The heat starts at the corners and then gradually works its way to the middle. The heat is slow but, over time, creates a subtle warmth.

"The control handset is easy to navigate, although it would benefit from a longer lead. Overall, I would say that this heated throw is warmish but not hot. It is probably worth the purchase to offset against keeping the central heating thermostat down."

An additional customer had a better experience, writing: "I can honestly say this is the best thing I’ve ever bought! My boiler had broken down, so I had no heating for a week. The heated throw arrived the next day and was a lifesaver! I have recommended it to everyone."

A final buyer of the StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw concluded: "I bought this to save money on heating bills. In the evening, I can turn the heating off (or down if it's very cold) and use this to keep warm. It does the trick! It has 9 heat settings and, so far, I've only used the lowest setting.

"It also has different options for automatic shut off. It's lovely and soft. I'm very pleased with it and with it being a Black Friday deal I got money off too. Highly recommended. There are different colours and styles available."

]]> Lakeland Lakeland adds a StaySnug electric throw to its Black Friday sale Lakeland adds a StaySnug electric throw to its Black Friday sale Lakeland adds a StaySnug electric throw to its Black Friday sale
markdown_content:
Lakeland has launched its [Black Friday event,](https://go.skimresources.com?id=76202X1561092&xs=1&xcust=Regionals-devonlive.com-10649558&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.lakeland.co.uk%2F63768%2Fstaysnug-sherpa-electric-heated-throw-grey ) featuring numerous reductions on winter-ready homeware, including electric blankets and throws. It's the ideal time to buy discounted heated throws, as temperatures are set to plummet this week.

One of the biggest savings is on a [StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw,](https://go.skimresources.com?id=76202X1561092&xs=1&xcust=Regionals-devonlive.com-10649558&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.lakeland.co.uk%2F63768%2Fstaysnug-sherpa-electric-heated-throw-grey ) which is reduced by £40 and is on sale for £49.99, from £89.99. The grey blanket has a soft fleece lining, nine heat settings and nine timer settings.

Measuring 120cm x 160cm, it can be used on beds and sofas for cosy evenings. The soft flannel outer layer of the blanket has a velvety, silver sheen.

- [**Shopping editor praises 'suitcase that never gets lost at airports' - now with 30% off**](https://www.devonlive.com/whats-on/shopping/nere-suitcase-black-friday-sale-10644068)
- [**M&S 'go-to' cable knit jumper that's a 'perfect fit' is under £30 in sale**](https://www.devonlive.com/whats-on/shopping/ms-go-to-cable-knit-10648979)

The [StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw](https://go.skimresources.com?id=76202X1561092&xs=1&xcust=Regionals-devonlive.com-10649558&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.lakeland.co.uk%2F63768%2Fstaysnug-sherpa-electric-heated-throw-grey ) features built-in overheating protection, allowing users to set the desired heat and time in advance. Similarly, Lakeland has also cut £10 off the [StaySnug Velvety Taupe Heated Throw](https://go.skimresources.com?id=76202X1561092&xs=1&xcust=Regionals-devonlive.com-10649558&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.lakeland.co.uk%2F75100%2Fstaysnug-velvety-taupe-heated-throw ), now £29.99, was £39.99.

Bedding brand Silentnight also has a variety of electric throws, including a [grey Luxury Heated Throw](https://go.skimresources.com?id=76202X1561092&xs=1&xcust=Regionals-devonlive.com-10649558&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boots.com%2Fsilentnight-luxury-heated-throw-grey-10338809 ) now on sale for £36, down from £45 at Boots. Also measuring 120cm x 160cm, it has nine heat settings, including a pre-heat option.

Meanwhile, this [Cosi Home electric throw](https://amzn.to/4i7Ah5B) has more than 12,000 customer reviews on Amazon. On sale for £40.79, the reversible blanket features warm microfleece fabric and a Sherpa reverse.

Lakeland's [Grey Sherpa Electric Heated Throw](https://go.skimresources.com?id=76202X1561092&xs=1&xcust=Regionals-devonlive.com-10649558&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.lakeland.co.uk%2F63768%2Fstaysnug-sherpa-electric-heated-throw-grey ) has garnered glowing reviews from purchasers, many of whom have complimented its warmth and ease of use. One person said: "Snug as a bug. Lovely soft blanket that heats up well."

Another shopper commented: "Excellent heat, great control. Lovely and cosy with fleece underside."

However, not all consumers were happy. One purchaser said it heats up slowly and had expected 'more of an instant heat'. They continued: "The heat starts at the corners and then gradually works its way to the middle. The heat is slow but, over time, creates a subtle warmth.

"The control handset is easy to navigate, although it would benefit from a longer lead. Overall, I would say that this heated throw is warmish but not hot. It is probably worth the purchase to offset against keeping the central heating thermostat down."

![StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw Grey, now £49.99 was £89.99](https://i2-prod.bristolpost.co.uk/incoming/article10649820.ece/ALTERNATES/s810/0_Screenshot-161.png "StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw Grey, now £49.99 was £89.99")

An additional customer had a better experience, writing: "I can honestly say this is the best thing I’ve ever bought! My boiler had broken down, so I had no heating for a week. The heated throw arrived the next day and was a lifesaver! I have recommended it to everyone."

A final buyer of the [StaySnug Sherpa Electric Heated Throw](https://go.skimresources.com?id=76202X1561092&xs=1&xcust=Regionals-devonlive.com-10649558&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.lakeland.co.uk%2F63768%2Fstaysnug-sherpa-electric-heated-throw-grey ) concluded: "I bought this to save money on heating bills. In the evening, I can turn the heating off (or down if it's very cold) and use this to keep warm. It does the trick! It has 9 heat settings and, so far, I've only used the lowest setting.

"It also has different options for automatic shut off. It's lovely and soft. I'm very pleased with it and with it being a Black Friday deal I got money off too. Highly recommended. There are different colours and styles available."
